rationale: The cited paper is used for more than a passing literature list mention. In the hypothesis development, the citing paper directly invokes Kashyap, Antia, and Frazier (2012) as evidence that incentives can keep franchisees compliant, which supports H4a about offsetting the motivation-dampening effect of higher royalty rates. It is also cited as prior franchise governance research and as methodological precedent for using CMP estimation, indicating an informing role in both framing and empirical setup.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on the cited paper as prior evidence and conceptual grounding for how franchise governance incentives affect franchisee behavior, then uses that logic to motivate its bankruptcy-focused hypotheses. It also references the cited study as an example of the estimation approach it employs.
